findings: stampede-monitor.sh aliases/swaps telemetry fields in ways that can silently change meaning (workers_completed falls back to atoms_received; workers_failed falls back to children_failed).
Monitor mixes telemetry and launch_proof fields with inconsistent naming (squad_leads_launched vs squad_leads_started; workers_launched vs workers_started).
Timestamp parsing is fragile; missing/variant formats fall back silently to “?”.
Incomplete-proof warning suppressed when workers_launched == 0 (falsy) and gated on status==success, so it can miss real failures during runtime.
Monitor does not consume child-agents.jsonl at all; proof ledger exists but monitor does not validate it.
Results display reads each result JSON multiple times via separate python invocations (mid-write inconsistency risk); runtime-stats updates swallow errors with bare except.
